---
title: "Finite-Window Rigidity in Fibonacci Numeration"
subtitle: "Symbolic dynamics and formal language theory"
date: 2026-04-07
categories: [gen2, symbolic, submitted]
journal: "Journal of Number Theory"
status: "Submitted"
repo: "the-omega-institute/resolution-folding-core-symbolic-dynamics"
theorems: 68
featured: false
description: "The Zeckendorf fold map is the normal-form map of a finite terminating confluent rewrite system, with a sharp block-bijection threshold at m=3. The right Fischer cover is identified as a suffix graph ..."
---

## Abstract

The Zeckendorf fold map is the normal-form map of a finite terminating confluent rewrite system, with a sharp block-bijection threshold at m=3. The right Fischer cover is identified as a suffix graph with 2^(m-1) states.

**Keywords:** Zeckendorf expansion, Fibonacci numeration, shifts of finite type, transducer theory, Markov chains

## Key Metrics

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Machine-verified theorems | 68 |
| Target journal | Journal of Number Theory |
| Status | Submitted |
| Scope | Symbolic dynamics and formal language theory |

## Video Explainer

*Video coming soon --- NotebookLM pipeline ready, YouTube upload pending.*

## Links

- [Full Paper (GitHub)](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/resolution-folding-core-symbolic-dynamics)
- [Lean 4 Proofs](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/automath/tree/dev-automation-integration/lean4)
- [Automath Engine](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/automath)
